_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in drafting serialized reports to support time-sensitive USCYBERCOM operations.
reference_document_element_description: Manage regulatory data reporting obligations, including data submission preparation, accuracy verification, submission execution, and confirmation documentation.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-004 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: LS-S-007 (LS, cosine: 0.449), TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.436).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.2/10 (raw 0.325). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [drafting, operations, reports, sensitive, serialized, skill] vs WIDAI-only terms [accuracy, confirmation, data, documentation, execution, manage] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.856 (P=0.853, R=0.859). Balanced P/R — the 10-word DCWF element and 16-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.6%). "Skill in drafting serialized reports to support time-sensitive USCYBERCOM operat" and "Manage regulatory data reporting obligations, including data submission preparat" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.451 > STS 0.325 (gap +0.125). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [drafting, operations, reports, sensitive, serialized, skill]. WIDAI terms: [accuracy, confirmation, data, documentation, execution, manage].
